text.title: in-progress → done gate (delegated)
text.summary: The promotion-gate methodology gates TWO lifecycle transitions. The `backlog → todo` gate is covered by 01-04 + 06. The `in-progress → done` gate is delegated to the readiness-checklist methodology and enforces additional quality gates.

rule.statement: For `in-progress → done` transitions, this methodology delegates to `readiness-checklist`. The readiness.md file MUST exist and every item ticked with evidence before the feature can be moved.
rule.rationale: The done-gate has 10 items with conditional applicability (API tests, Playwright, UI heuristics). Replicating that logic here would drift; single source of truth lives in readiness-checklist.

section.title: Additional quality-gate items enforced at the done gate

list.item: API tests pass — REQUIRED when the feature touched backend code (any file under backend source tree).
Playwright positive AND negative cases pass — REQUIRED when the feature has a user-facing flow.
Surface-coupling check — REQUIRED when the feature added a new public surface (skill trigger, API path, CLI flag, exported name).

rule.statement: The reviewer MUST verify which conditionals apply by inspecting the git diff, NOT by accepting the developer's self-assessment.
rule.rationale: The author has a blind spot for what counts as "user-facing" or "public surface". The diff is the source of truth.

antipattern.description: readiness.md exists but several items are blank or marked "n/a" without justification.
antipattern.reason: Blank ≠ pass. The done gate is binary; blanks block.

antipattern.description: API tests skipped because "the change was small" although a backend file changed.
antipattern.reason: The conditional is on the diff, not on the developer's estimate of impact.

text.reference: Canonical 10-item readiness checklist; this gate consumes it.
